# The 11 Best OKR & Goal Setting Software of 2026: We Tested the Top Platforms (AppIntent)

## Key findings

### Tool positioning (as of 2026)

| Tool | Best for | Starting price | Key differentiator |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Lattice** | People ops goals + OKRs integrated with performance management | $11/user/month | Deep PM+OKR integration; learning curve noted |
| **15Five** | Check-ins with goal tracking | $4/user/month | Lightweight check-in workflow primary; OKRs secondary |
| **Weekdone** | Lightweight OKRs and check-ins | $10-12/user/month | Simplicity; 14-day free trial |
| **Notion** | OKR tracking via templates | Free (with limitations) | Not a dedicated OKR platform; requires manual setup |

### What to look for in 2026 OKR software

Best-in-class features:
- Visual goal alignment trees (company → team → individual)
- Customizable dashboards
- Automated progress updates through integrations
- Built-in check-in tools
- Robust reporting capabilities
- Nested OKRs (company → department → individual)
- Confidence scoring
- KPI dashboards alongside OKR view

### 2026 market trends

- OKR software increasingly bridges goal progress with **time investment tracking** (how much effort maps to each OKR)
- Supports **async-first workflows** for remote teams
- Focus on **connecting OKRs to daily work** rather than existing as isolated documents
- Most reviews emphasize that "successful OKR adoption depends on consistent visibility and lightweight check-in processes rather than complex administrative overhead"

### Lattice specifics (2026)

- Positions as an all-in-one platform: performance reviews + OKRs + engagement + compensation
- OKR module supports company → department → team → individual alignment
- Users note: "deep integration with performance management is a strength but creates complexity for teams that only want OKRs"
- Review cycles can be connected to OKR progress scores (caution: compensation linkage risk)

### 15Five specifics (2026)

- Originally a weekly check-in tool that added OKR tracking
- OKRs are secondary to the check-in cadence in the product design
- Well-suited for teams where the check-in habit already exists and goal tracking is additive
- $4/user/month makes it the budget leader

### Notion OKR templates (2026)

- Multiple community templates available (free)
- Common structure: Objectives database + Key Results database + linked relations
- Requires manual status updates (no automation)
- Better for small teams with low OKR maturity or teams already living in Notion
- "Notion OKR template" search returns dozens of community-built options

## Relevance to okr-goal-setting-stinger

- **guides/07-tools.md**: The comparison table and tool-specific notes are the backbone of this guide. Warn about Lattice's compensation-linkage risk (OKR scores visible in performance reviews).
- **guides/06-small-team-adaptation.md**: For small teams, Weekdone or Notion template is the recommended starting point; Lattice is overkill below ~50 people.
- Note in the stinger that tool UXs change frequently — these configurations should be verified against current docs, not taken as permanent (per Command Brief critical directive)
- The "connects OKRs to daily work" trend validates the weekly check-in emphasis in the cadence guide
